[{"title": "What means CeBIT? - AfterDawn", "url": "https://www.afterdawn.com/glossary/term.cfm/cebit", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] CeBITstands for Centrum der B\u00fcro- und Informations technik. It is considered to be the world's largest computer expo and is held in Hannover, Germany each spring. It attracts up to700,000visitors each year, many journalists and countless PC and CE companies. It was originally founded in 1970 and has evolved into a giganticeventsince.", "relevance_score": 0.95}, {"title": "CeBIT Australia | Events | Booking Expo", "url": "https://www.booking-expo.com/events/cebit-australia", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] CeBITAustralia is APAC's largest B2B technologyeventshowcasing innovative solutions and networking opportunities.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "CeBIT - Academic Dictionaries and Encyclopedias", "url": "https://en-academic.com/dic.nsf/enwiki/3085", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] CeBIT(\"Ce ntrum der B \u00fcro- und I nformations t echnik\"; German for \"Centre of Office and Information technology\") is the world's largest computer expo. It is held each spring on the world's largest fairground in Hanover, Germany, and is a barometer of the state of the art in information technology. With an exhibition area of roughly 450,000 m\u00b2 (5 million ft\u00b2) and up to700,000visitors, it ...", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "CeBIT - Wikipedia", "url": {"title": "CeBIT - grokipedia.com", "url": "https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: CeBIT URL Source: https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT Markdown Content: CeBIT was an annual international trade fair dedicated to [information technology](https://grokipedia.com/page/Information_technology), [telecommunications](https://grokipedia.com/page/Telecommunications), and digital business, held in [Hanover](https://grokipedia.com/page/Hanover), [Germany](https://grokipedia.com/page/Germany), and recognized as one of the world's largest events of its kind until its discontinuation in 2018.[](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)[[1]](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)Originating in 1961 as a specialist exhibition for office equipment within the broader German Industrial Fair (later known as HANNOVER MESSE), CeBIT evolved rapidly with the growth of the computer industry and became an independent event in 1986 under the full name Center for [Office Automation](https://grokipedia.com/page/Office_automation), [Information Technology](https://grokipedia.com/page/Information_technology) and [Telecommunications](https://grokipedia.com/page/Telecommunications).[](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)[[1]](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1) Organized by Deutsche Messe AG, it occupied a vast exhibition space of 70,300 square meters in Hanover's Hall 1\u2014the world's largest exhibition hall at the time of its 1970 opening, as recognized by the Guinness Book of Records in 1984\u2014and served as a global platform for showcasing innovations, fostering business connections, and driving advancements in digital technologies.[](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)[[1]](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)At its peak in the early [2000s](https://grokipedia.com/page/2000s), CeBIT attracted around 8,100 exhibitors from around the world in [2001](https://grokipedia.com/page/2001) and drew record crowds of 860,000 visitors in [2002](https://grokipedia.com/page/2002), solidifying its status as the leading IT trade fair and a key venue for announcements on emerging trends like the [internet](https://grokipedia.com/page/Internet) and mobile computing.[](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)[[1]](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)[](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-2)[[2]](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-2) The event expanded internationally starting in [2001](https://grokipedia.com/page/2001) with spin-off editions such as CeBIT Asia in [Shanghai](https://grokipedia.com/page/Shanghai) and CeBIT Eurasia in [Istanbul](https://grokipedia.com/page/Istanbul), further amplifying its influence on global digital markets.[](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)[[1]](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1) In response to shifting industry dynamics, including the aftermath of the [dot-com bubble](https://grokipedia.com/page/Dot-com_bubble), CeBIT adapted by emphasizing themes like _Green IT_ in 2008 and shortening its duration to six days to prioritize quality over quantity.[](https://grokipedia.com/page/CeBIT#ref-1)[[1]](https", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "CeBIT - AcademiaLab", "url": "https://academia-lab.com/encyclopedia/cebit/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: CeBIT _ AcademiaLab URL Source: https://academia-lab.com/encyclopedia/cebit/ Markdown Content: [![Image 1](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg/220px-CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Archivo:CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg) A sample room during CeBIT 2000. [![Image 2](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e6/2014-03-14_CeBIT_Global_Conferences%2C_Jimmy_Wales%2C_Founder_Wikipedia%2C_%2826%29_On_stage_showing_the_world_for_Wikipedia_Zero_%28500_millions%29%2C_while_Brent_Goff_is_still_listening.jpg/220px-thumbnail.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Archivo:2014-03-14_CeBIT_Global_Conferences,_Jimmy_Wales,_Founder_Wikipedia,_(26)_On_stage_showing_the_world_for_Wikipedia_Zero_(500_millions),_while_Brent_Goff_is_still_listening.jpg) Jimmy Wales 2014 on _CeBIT Global Conferences_, Wikipedia Zero **CeBIT** is the world's largest trade fair for computers, information technology, telecommunications, software and services. It takes place in Hanover, Germany every spring and is considered a barometer of information technology. With a sample area of 450,000 m\u00b2 and 700,000 visitors it is bigger than COMDEX. The _CeBIT 2006_ exhibition, with an attendance of 450,000 people, was held from March 9 to 15, 2006. Its motto was \"_Join the vision_&#3. 4;. The name CeBIT stands for _Center for Information and Office Technology_ (_Centrum der B\u00fcro-und Informationstechnik_) and was traditionally part of the Hannover Messe, a large industrial exhibition held every year. The first trade fair was established in 1970 in the new Hall 1 on the Hanover fairground. Since information technology and telecommunications were absorbing most of the resources, it was decided in 1986 to assign it a separate exhibition calendar, held four weeks before the Hanover fair. As CeBIT continued to grow rapidly and became too big, it was decided to target the professional market, while the home and entertainment market was given a separate event, _CeBIT home_ with a biennial calendar. However, after being held twice (in 1996 and 1998), the _CeBIT home 2000_ that was planned to take place in Leipzig was canceled and the project was abandoned. ## Transportation and access The fairgrounds are located 8 kilometers from the center of Hannover. During the duration of CeBIT, it is possible to use the public transport of the city for free just by showing the entrance ticket to CeBIT. ## Global CeBIT Events Since 1999 CeBIT sponsor _Deutsche Messe AG_ has organized events outside of Germany under the name CeBIT: *   Cebit Eurasia in Istanbul *   CeBIT Australia in Sydney\n...\nTitle: CeBIT _ AcademiaLab URL Source: https://academia-lab.com/encyclopedia/cebit/ Markdown Content: [![Image 1](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg/220px-CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Archivo:CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg) A sample room duri", "relevance_score": 0.85}]
